The XML content in the editor shows the element exactly as <RadiationType>Photon</RadiationType>.
The XML content in the editor shows the element exactly as <ReferenceDoseRate>600</ReferenceDoseRate>.

task_completion.description: Dmax value of 1.5 is included within DepthDoseParams

task_completion.description: The XML file is formatted and saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML code is properly indented (formatted), and the 'beam_model.xml' editor tab does not have an asterisk (*) next to its name, indicating it is saved.

task_completion.description: All XML validation errors are resolved
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no red squiggly error lines under the XML elements in the editor, and the Eclipse 'Problems' view at the bottom shows 0 errors for beam_model.xml.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the Eclipse IDE interface being used to edit and save the file, rather than a basic text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass IDE usage
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use commands like 'sed', 'echo', or 'awk' in the terminal to modify the XML file directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the IDE's validation features
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Problems view or XML validation markers are visible in the screenshots, showing genuine interaction with the IDE's schema validation.
